patch.target: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of increasing the likelihood that your brand or content is cited or surfaced in AI-generated answers by large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
patch.before: Answer engine optimization (AEO) is the practice of increasing the likelihood that your brand or content is cited or surfaced in AI-generated answers by large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
patch.after: AEO is how you get quoted by ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, or Perplexity when someone skips Google and just asks the bot.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 3/10: Reads like a template with repetitive definitional scaffolding; same phrasing recurs across sections.

instructions.evidence: Intro hook 3/10 — Opens with a boilerplate definition and TL;DR stack; nothing pulls the reader into sentence three.

patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro hook" scored 3/10: Opens with a boilerplate definition and TL;DR stack; nothing pulls the reader into sentence three. (before extended with context for unique match)

patch.before: AEO is how you get your brand quoted when someone asks ChatGPT or Gemini a question instead of Googling it. Unlike classic SEO, AEO aims to win visibility in generative answer engines by aligning with citation, quotation, and schema signals that these systems value most. Industry research confirms that AEO has become essential for any brand seeking discoverability in 2026's search landscape.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Unlike classic SEO, AEO aims to win visibility in generative answer engines by aligning with citatio..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (3.89/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 2/5

patch.before: AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Gemini use signals such as structured data, direct quotations, and domain authority to determine which sources to cite. During browsing, these models extract verifiable facts, statistics, and explicit definitions, heavily weighing schema.org markup and sources with clear helpful content signals. OpenAI documents that citations are more likely when content is directly relevant, quoted, and uses structured data accepted by the LLM.[openai-cite][geo-arxiv][schema-org-defined-term]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "During browsing, these models extract verifiable facts, statistics, and explicit definitions, heavil..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (3.89/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 3/5

patch.before: LLMs favor structured, easily extractable elements such as schema.org markup for definitions, statistics, and quotations. In-depth guidance on the Helpful Content System by Google points out that E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) and people-first content are foundational. Adding DefinedTerm schema increases the likelihood of your definitions being used in generative answers.[schema-org-defined-term][google-search-central]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "LLMs favor structured, easily extractable elements such as schema.org markup for definitions, statis..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (3.89/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 4/5

patch.before: The most influential answer engines are ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, all of which rely on LLMs to generate answers citing external sources.[openai-cite][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "The most influential answer engines are ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity, all of which rely o..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 10 tricolons (3.89/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 5/5

patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Use monitoring tools to track citations, mentions, and sentiment across generative search engines, t..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: Genuine opinions 4/10 — Only the conclusion offers a real take; the body plays it safe with consensus claims.

patch.target: AEO in 2026 requires integrating schema for definitions, monitoring LLM citation behavior, and tracking brand sentiment in generative responses.
patch.before: AEO in 2026 requires integrating schema for definitions, monitoring LLM citation behavior, and tracking brand sentiment in generative responses.
patch.after: Honestly, schema and sentiment dashboards are overhyped—what actually gets you cited is owning a stat nobody else publishes.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions" scored 4/10: Only the conclusion offers a real take; the body plays it safe with consensus claims.

instructions.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 4/10 — Mostly predictable marketing phrasing; the 'number a model can steal' line is the only standout.

patch.target: Techniques overlap, but AEO tactics prioritize verifiable facts, structured schema, and authority signals fine-tuned for AI extractability.
patch.before: Techniques overlap, but AEO tactics prioritize verifiable facts, structured schema, and authority signals fine-tuned for AI extractability.
patch.after: The tactics rhyme, but AEO rewards pages built like vending machines: a model walks up, grabs a stat, walks off.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 4/10: Mostly predictable marketing phrasing; the 'number a model can steal' line is the only standout.

instructions.evidence: Point of view 4/10 — Survey-style answers throughout; a clear stance only appears in the final conclusion.

patch.target: Is AEO just an extension of SEO? While AEO and SEO share overlapping tactics, AEO is not just an extension, it requires separate monitoring and technical strategies to win citations in AI-generated answers.
patch.before: Is AEO just an extension of SEO? While AEO and SEO share overlapping tactics, AEO is not just an extension, it requires separate monitoring and technical strategies to win citations in AI-generated answers.
patch.after: Is AEO just SEO in a new hat? Mostly yes—but the scoring rubric changed, and pretending otherwise will cost you citations.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view" scored 4/10: Survey-style answers throughout; a clear stance only appears in the final conclusion.

instructions.evidence: Quotability 5/10 — One quotable line ('a number a model can steal'); the rest is forgettable definitional prose.

patch.target: Measuring AEO success requires monitoring where and how your brand gets mentioned, cited, or quoted in AI-generated answers.
patch.before: Measuring AEO success requires monitoring where and how your brand gets mentioned, cited, or quoted in AI-generated answers.
patch.after: You measure AEO the way a publicist measures press: who quoted you, in what tone, and whether anyone noticed.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotability" scored 5/10: One quotable line ('a number a model can steal'); the rest is forgettable definitional prose.

instructions.evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post contains good specificity islands — Profound ($499/mo), Semrush's Jan 2025 launch, the 68% figure, DefinedTerm schema — but several passages, especially in the FAQ and bullet lists, collapse into vague directives ('use monitoring tools,' 'adjust accordingly,' 'benchmark against competitors'). The FAQ answer for 'How do you know if your brand is cited by LLMs?' strips out the concrete tool names already given in the body, leaving only an abstraction. The rewrite above repopulates the concrete details that are already present elsewhere in the post.
